content:
Day 27
MORNING
  • Morning Reading: "From that hour on, Siddhartha ceased to fight against his destiny. There shone in his face the serenity of knowledge... who has found salvation, who is in harmony with the stream of events, with the stream of life... surrendering himself to the stream, belonging to the unity of all things."
  • Today's Intention: I will perform one act of service today, however small, as a gesture of my connection to the whole.
NIGHT
  • Night Reading: "Vasudeva rose. ‘I have been waiting for this hour, my friend. Now that it has come, let me go. For a long time I have been Vasudeva the ferryman; now it is over. Farewell, hut, farewell, river, farewell, Siddhartha!’"
  • Reflection: When did I feel most connected to the world and people around me today?
Day 28
MORNING
  • Morning Reading: "‘I have come to ask you for a word of teaching,’ the old man said... ‘I have sought all my life, I have been a seeker, but I have not yet found what I was looking for.’"
context: From document: A 30-Day Journey with Siddhartha: Aligning the Path with Daily Life — chunk 26 (chunk 26/29)
